titleStmt.title: Two letters from Lord Cavan to John Redmond, 25 January & 8 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Frederick Rudolph Lambart

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Two short letters from Frederick Rudolph Lambart, 10th Earl of Cavan (1865-1946). Lord Cavan joined the Grenadier Guards and fought in the Boer War, retired from the army in 1913, only to be recalled at the start of the Great War. He was elected a representative peer for Ireland in 1915 (one of the last to be elected), and was appointed head of the XIV Corps of British Expeditionary Force in January 1916. The letters are written to John Redmond (1856-1918) MP and leader of the Irish Parliamentary Party at Westminster from 1900-1918 and refers to his unit and thanks Redmond for his support.

correspAction.placeName: Halsey Street, Cadogan Gardens, London, England

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Lord CAVAN to R Feb 8 '16 HQ 14th Corps BEF The Guards will be a part of my Corps directly I need not sa
how del igh ted I am ..... I am sure the demand for reinforce
ments is bound to be very great by the autumn ad if we CAN reinf
force when Germany CANT we shal have made a big step forward v truly yrs Cavan Jan 25 '16 Dear Mr Redmond Thank you for your little book and the
kind things said about the Guards and myself I now command the 14th Corps and thisicludes the Ulster divn Tey have not b een
tried yet in action but they are full of zeal and energy and their
disci p line seems good It is unutterably sad to me that it shd have bn necessary to excl u de Ireland from its sha e of our common
burden and I feel sure that although we probably differ very
cannotbe wrong to aim at it Nobody really wd ENJOY this
fighting more than the men of the South and South West if they wd but try it Yrs v truly Cavan
47, Halsey Street,
Cadogan Gardens, S.W.S.
